compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
`tests/scripts/graph/test_graph_perf.py::test_graph_build_scales_linearly` asserts `ratio < 3.0` for build_dep_graph wall-clock at n=1000 vs n=2000. On a loaded/variable macOS GitHub-hosted runner the ratio can exceed 3.0 with no real regression (observed 3.64: t(1000)=0.919s, t(2000)=3.349s), producing a spurious Verified -1. It red-lit an unrelated docs-only change (Gerrit 511, CLAUDE.md).

## Reproduction Steps
1. Run the default suite on a macOS GitHub-hosted runner under load.
2. Observe intermittent FAIL of test_graph_build_scales_linearly with ratio just over 3.0.
Expected: a linear-scaling check that is robust to runner timing noise.
Actual: a hard wall-clock-ratio threshold that flakes on shared runners.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The scaling check no longer flakes on macOS CI (e.g. larger n for a stabler ratio, best-of-k timing, a looser/adaptive threshold, or an operation-count proxy instead of wall-clock).
- [ ] It still catches a genuine super-linear regression.
- [ ] Distinct in class from the git-add store-write flake (edf7/73d8) — this is a timing-ratio assertion, not the object-DB retry path.
compiled_state.title: Flaky perf test test_graph_build_scales_linearly — timing-ratio assertion trips on slow macOS CI runners
